title: The New Statutes

title: The Heavy Ground
excerpt: I wanted to paint the moment after the noise has died — when the only sound is the wind moving through torn metal. The earth here remembers weight, and I tried to let the paint carry that memory. These fields were never…

title: The Quiet Before the Rupture
excerpt: I remember the stillness of that morning, the way the light fell across the empty parade ground. Then the air itself seemed to tear. I wanted to paint not the blast, but the silence that preceded it—the ordinary things…

title: Convoy by the Trent, MDCXLIII
excerpt: There is a kind of quiet in the dark before a crossing. I wanted to show that pause — the weight of wool and iron, the cold breath of horses, the river moving past everything. These moments are not recorded in dispatche…

title: The Comet's Rest
excerpt: I paint what remains after silence falls. The mountain does not speak of what it took, but the light remembers the curve of a wing, the sudden absence of sound. I wanted to capture that moment when the sky closes behind…

title: The Naming of Distant Waters
excerpt: I was thinking about how we reach into the dark and try to give things names, as if that will make them less strange. There is a tenderness in that gesture, a kind of hope. I wanted to paint the moment just before a nam…

title: The Listener, MCMLXXIX
excerpt: I remember the first time I heard music that was mine alone. The world receded into a soft blur, leaving only the voice in my ear. This painting is an attempt to capture that moment of isolation and intimacy.

title: The Picket Line's Dawn
excerpt: I was drawn to the quiet resolve in their eyes. The morning light caught the creases in their coats, each one a story of waiting. These are the hands that hold a city together, even when the city forgets them.

title: The Gathering of Fifty
